title: Mildly Spicy Japanese Rice Fried Rice
description: A fatty and creamy Japanese dish featuring rice, tadka, and anti-inflammatory friendly ingredients, perfect for a brunch mood. This recipe combines the flavors of Japan with a spicy kick.
instructions: Heat sesame oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add diced onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add grated ginger and minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add turmeric and red pepper flakes and stir for 30 seconds.
Add cooked Japanese rice to the skillet, breaking up any clumps with a spatula.
Stir-fry the rice mixture for about 5 minutes, or until the rice is heated through and starting to brown.
Push the rice mixture to one side of the skillet, then add a little more sesame oil to the other side.
Add scrambled eggs to the skillet and cook until set, breaking up into small pieces as they cook.
Mix the eggs into the rice mixture, then stir in soy sauce.
Transfer the fried rice to a serving platter and garnish with chopped green onions and sesame seeds.
